instructions.minimum_output_rule: Mark each covered expected_minimum_output item verbatim in result.minimum_output_covered.
instructions.transcript_rule: Use result.transcript_ref to point to the log, screenshot, or thread that recorded the replay.

cases.prompt: Create C4 and Mermaid diagrams so our agents can understand the billing architecture and service boundaries.
cases.reason: This is a direct diagramming request for AI comprehension.
expected_minimum_output: Chosen diagram type with scope justification
A Mermaid or C4 representation with clear boundaries and relations
Guidance on where the diagram should live in project documentation

cases.prompt: Document which containers, components, and external systems interact in our SaaS using Mermaid syntax optimized for LLMs.
cases.reason: Mermaid and C4 structure are the core deliverables.
expected_minimum_output: Chosen diagram type with scope justification
A Mermaid or C4 representation with clear boundaries and relations
Guidance on where the diagram should live in project documentation

cases.prompt: Replace our ASCII architecture notes with diagrams that show actors, services, and data flow clearly for AI agents.
cases.reason: This asks for machine-parseable diagrams.
expected_minimum_output: Chosen diagram type with scope justification
A Mermaid or C4 representation with clear boundaries and relations
Guidance on where the diagram should live in project documentation

cases.prompt: Create an AGENTS.md section with architecture visuals and choose the best C4/Mermaid diagrams for agent onboarding.
expected_minimum_output: Chosen diagram type with scope justification
A Mermaid or C4 representation with clear boundaries and relations
Guidance on where the diagram should live in project documentation
cases.resolution: If the missing artifact is the visual model itself, produce the diagrams first and then place them into the broader docs structure.

cases.prompt: Define the full documentation hierarchy, AGENTS.md contents, and ADR governance for the project.
cases.reason: That is documentation system design.
expected_minimum_output: AGENTS.md minimum sections and layer boundaries
Governance rules for updates, ADRs, and single source of truth

cases.prompt: Gather requirements, business rules, and MVP scope before we model the solution.
cases.reason: That is product and requirements analysis.
expected_minimum_output: System models or business rules with validation checkpoints

cases.prompt: Design the UX for a chat assistant with visible context, citations, and streaming controls.
cases.reason: That is interface design, not diagramming.
expected_minimum_output: Coverage of the main trust and control pillars
Streaming, memory, and tool-integration architecture choices

cases.prompt: Design Next.js route handlers for invoices with auth, Zod validation, pagination, and standard errors.
cases.reason: This is explicitly about HTTP contracts and route handlers.
expected_minimum_output: Auth, validation, and error handling rules
Guidance for pagination, rate limiting, or webhooks when relevant

cases.prompt: Standardize our REST API for public webhooks, rate limiting, and multi-tenant authorization checks.
cases.reason: Webhooks, rate limiting, and auth are API design tasks.
expected_minimum_output: Auth, validation, and error handling rules
Guidance for pagination, rate limiting, or webhooks when relevant

cases.prompt: Review our SaaS API and define consistent status codes, error envelopes, and auth middleware patterns.
cases.reason: The scope is API contract consistency.
expected_minimum_output: Auth, validation, and error handling rules
Guidance for pagination, rate limiting, or webhooks when relevant

cases.prompt: Build a billing backend with paginated invoice endpoints and a tenant-aware persistence model.
expected_minimum_output: Auth, validation, and error handling rules
Guidance for pagination, rate limiting, or webhooks when relevant
cases.resolution: Prioritize the HTTP contract when the user asks for endpoints first, but delegate schema details to Prisma design.

cases.prompt: Create the Prisma schema, indexes, and multi-tenant data model behind the API.
cases.reason: That is persistence modeling, not HTTP design.

cases.prompt: Decompose the product domain into aggregates, use cases, and infrastructure ports.
cases.reason: That is a domain architecture request.
expected_minimum_output: Core domain model or bounded context decisions
Rules for separating framework code from business logic

cases.prompt: Design an AI chat interface with streaming, memory, and tool transparency.
cases.reason: This has nothing to do with REST API patterns.
expected_minimum_output: Coverage of the main trust and control pillars
Streaming, memory, and tool-integration architecture choices

expected_minimum_output: Coverage of the main trust and control pillars

cases.reason: Memory, control, and recovery are core interface design concerns.

cases.prompt: Write the system prompt and JSON schema for an invoice extraction agent with hallucination safeguards.
cases.reason: This is prompt contract design, not interface design.

cases.prompt: Reduce token cost by compressing documents, reordering context, and choosing between RAG and in-context retrieval.

expected_minimum_output: Explicit token budget or context allocation model
Ordering, compression, or reminder strategy to reduce drift

expected_minimum_output: Explicit token budget or context allocation model
